steps.title: Read the naming phrase
steps.content: The blank appears inside a pair of dashes after including the. The sentence needs the phrase languages Javanese and Bikol, where Javanese and Bikol directly name which languages are being discussed. This is an appositive-style naming relationship within the same phrase. Because the names identify the noun immediately, no punctuation should separate languages from those names.

steps.title: Reject extra punctuation
steps.content: Choice A keeps the identifying phrase intact. Choice B adds a colon after wording that is not a complete independent clause. Choice C inserts another dash inside an already dashed parenthetical phrase, disrupting the structure. Choice D places a comma between the noun and the language names that identify it. The sentence reads smoothly only without added punctuation after languages. The answer is A.
